__comment: Grafana dashboard model (importable) showing PRODUCT-ADJACENT traffic shape from the EXISTING Prometheus series at /api/metrics. This is a stopgap until an event analytics backend (funnel.json / retention.json) is connected: until then the only product signal in the metrics pipeline is request traffic on product routes (the analytics collector POST, the challenge actions, the paywall). Series come from apps/web/lib/metrics.ts: http_requests_total{route,status} and http_request_duration_ms{route}. Import via Grafana -> Dashboards -> New -> Import, then select your Prometheus data source. The `route` label values shown (e.g. 'POST /api/analytics') depend on what call sites pass to handleRoute(); unlabeled routes fall under route='unknown'. See docs/ANALYTICS.md and docs/OBSERVABILITY.md.
title: Project 50 — Product traffic (Prometheus stopgap)

panels.title: Analytics events received (POST /api/analytics)
panels.description: Throughput of the analytics collector endpoint — a proxy for tracked-event volume reaching the server until a real sink drains window.p50Analytics.

panels.title: Challenge action traffic
panels.description: Request rate on the routes behind starting a challenge and toggling rules — server-side echo of project50_started / rule_toggled activity. Adapt route names to whatever the call sites pass to handleRoute().

panels.title: Paywall / billing traffic
panels.description: Request rate on upgrade/checkout routes — server-side echo of upgrade_clicked intent and Stripe Checkout starts.

panels.title: Product-route error rate %
panels.description: 5xx share on the product routes above — errors here directly break funnel steps (a failed challenge-start or checkout is a lost conversion).
